The anti-K-εGG antibody (monoclonal, raised against the Gly-Gly-modified lysine) immunoprecipitates these peptides from the digest. The commercial standard is Cell Signaling’s PTMScan
Ubiquitin Remnant Motif Kit. The foundational papers are Xu 2010 Nat Biotechnol (Bennett et al.,
Gygi lab) and Kim 2011 Mol Cell (Kim et al., Finley/Gygi lab), which independently established
the workflow and demonstrated >10,000 sites identifiable in a single experiment.
[FIGURE: K-εGG enrichment workflow — Lysis with DUB inhibitors (NEM, iodoacetamide, PR619) ￿ 8M urea denaturation ￿ reduction, alkylation, dilution ￿ tryptic digest ￿ anti-K-εGG IP
￿ LC-MS/MS with TMT-SPS-MS3 ￿ site-level quantification]
Practical Protocol (the student should know these numbers)
Parameter Typical value
Input protein 10–20 mg per condition
Lysis buffer 8 M urea, 50 mM Tris pH 8, protease + DUB inhibitors
DUB inhibitors NEM (5 mM), iodoacetamide, PR-619 (50 µM)
Reduction/alkylation 5 mM DTT then 14 mM IAA
Digest LysC 4h then trypsin overnight at 1:100 enzyme:protein
Antibody 31 µL PTMScan per ~10 mg
Pre-MS fractionation High-pH reverse-phase C18 (offline)
TMT labeling AFTER IP (on IP eluate) for multiplexing

Critical Failure Modes and Controls
• Loss during lysis : endogenous DUBs strip ubiquitin quickly after cell lysis. NEM and iodoacetamide covalently inactivate cysteine-dependent DUBs; PR-619 is a broad reversible DUB inhibitor; lysis at 95 °C in urea is a further safeguard.
• Denaturation: 8 M urea disrupts complexes and denatures all enzyme activity , including DUBs.
• TMT order of operations : tag AFTER IP because NHS-ester TMT labels the ε-amine of all
lysines, including the Gly-Gly-modified lysine, changing antibody recognition.
• Offline fractionation : high-pH reverse-phase C18 fractionation of the IP eluate before MS
roughly doubles site identifications.
Limitations
1. NEDD8 and ISG15 cross-reactivity — the single most important caveat and the subject of M10
below. NEDD8 also ends in LRGG; its tryptic remnant on substrate lysines is also GG (114
Da) and is indistinguishable from ubiquitin in standard K-εGG data. ISG15 ends in LRLRGG
and also leaves a GG remnant after trypsin. In IFN-treated or interferon-stimulated-gene-high
contexts (exactly the biology of this thesis), ISG15 contamination is a real concern.
2. Compressed dynamic range — especially with TMT co-isolation. SPS-MS3 is essential.
3. Stoichiometry not measured — K-εGG tells you a site is modified but not what fraction of the
substrate is modified. Absolute stoichiometry requires SILAC pulses or AQUA peptides.
4. Chain linkage not directly read — standard K-εGG tells you the substrate lysine that is modified, but distinguishing K48, K63, K11, K6, K27, K29, K33 ubiquitin chains on the substrate
requires Ub-AQUA of ubiquitin-peptide spectra themselves (Kirkpatrick 2006, Phu 2011).

sections.title: Buckley 2012 Cell Stem Cell — The Hematopoietic Ubiquitinomics Paradigm

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
Buckley, Aranda-Orgilles, Strikoudis et al., 2012, Cell Stem Cell , from the Aifantis lab, is the
single most cited paper for K-GG proteomics in a stem/progenitor context and the student’s committee will expect intimate familiarity .
Summary: The authors applied K-εGG proteomics to mouse embryonic stem cells, induced
pluripotent stem cells and fibroblasts and identified 2,578 ubiquitination sites across 1,168
proteins, many of them pluripotency-relevant. Key biological findings included:
• Identification of FBXW7-cMyc phospho-degron (Thr58) as a modulator of pluripotency , tied
to the MYC network that drives both iPSC reprogramming and leukemic self-renewal.
• Demonstration of PSMD14 (Rpn11) as a proteasome-intrinsic DUB essential for pluripotency ,
establishing the principle that DUB activity at the proteasome, not just E3 activity , is ratelimiting in stem-cell proteostasis.
• Broad mapping of ubiquitinated substrates of pluripotency-regulating E3s.
Why the committee cares: The paper is the paradigm for what a “good K-εGG experiment”
looks like in a hematopoietic-adjacent context. It sets the quantitative and analytical expectations
(replicate number, FDR, orthogonal validation). The student should be able to name the scale

(2,578/1,168), the FBXW7-cMyc finding, and the method (SILAC + K-εGG IP + Orbitrap) from
memory .

sections.title: Identifying CRL Substrates — The Orthogonal T oolkit

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
No single experiment defines a CRL substrate. A convincing call requires orthogonal lines of evidence. The following are the tools the field uses; Aim 1b will draw from several.
Direct: K-εGG DiGly Proteomics
Discussed in M4. Directly identifies modified lysines and, in quantitative formats, changes in
ubiquitination state between conditions (e.g., DCAF7-WT vs DCAF7-KO).
MLN4924 / Pevonedistat Comparison
MLN4924 (pevonedistat) inhibits the NEDD8-activating enzyme NAE (Soucy et al., 2009 Nature).
Because all cullin-RING ligases require NEDD8 conjugation of their cullin scaffold for activity ,
NAE inhibition collapses all CRL E3 activity and stabilizes ALL CRL substrates globally . Emanuele
et al., 2011 Cell paired MLN4924 with TMT proteomics and systematically nominated hundreds
of CRL substrates across the proteome. This logic is directly portable to Aim 1b: IFIT3 should
accumulate under MLN4924, just as it does under DCAF7 loss.
Genetic Loss-of-Function (the student’s approach)
Compare DCAF7-WT vs DCAF7-KO HEL cells by whole-proteome and K-εGG proteomics; bona
fide substrates accumulate (proteomics) and lose ubiquitination (K-εGG). This is the primary Aim
1b design.
dTAG / AID Acute Degradation
Nabet 2018 Nat Chem Biol established the dTAG system: a FKBP12-F36V fusion that is acutely
degraded by a heterobifunctional small molecule (dTAG-13 or dTAGV-1). Replacing genetic KO
with acute dTAG-DCAF7 degradation has three advantages: (i) cleaner kinetics because substrates
are surveyed within hours of DCAF7 loss, before compensatory transcriptional adaptation; (ii)
elimination of long-term selection artifacts in KO clones; (iii) time-resolved substrate detection. An
analogous auxin-inducible degron (AID/AUX) strategy is a viable alternative. Proposing dTAGDCAF7 as a companion line to the KO is a strong direction to pitch in the oral.
Pulse-SILAC / SILAC-TMT Half-Life Measurements
Schwanhäusser 2011 Nature and Cambridge 2011 J Proteome Res demonstrated global half-life
measurement by pulsing heavy SILAC amino acids and tracking the replacement of light with

heavy species over time. This yields direct protein-level t1/2 proteome-wide. Combining pulseSILAC with DCAF7-WT vs KO directly tests whether IFIT3 half-life extends in the KO, as the
student’s single-protein CHX chase already predicts.
BioID / TurboID Proximity Labeling
Fusing DCAF7 to TurboID (a promiscuous biotin ligase; May 2020 Curr Opin Chem Biol) and
pulsing biotin labels all proteins within ~10 nm. Streptavidin pulldown + MS identifies proximal
proteins, which include substrates AND stable interactors AND neighbors — it is not ubiquitinspecific. Useful for nominating the DCAF7 interactome as a filter for K-εGG hits.
Co-IP/AP-MS of DCAF7
Classic affinity purification of tagged DCAF7 followed by MS; identifies stable interactors including DDB1, CUL4, and potentially substrates if crosslinked or trapped in the pre-ubiquitination
complex. Gao et al., 2015 eLife used this approach and was foundational in placing DCAF7 in the
DDB1-CUL4 adaptor family .
TUBE (T andem Ubiquitin Binding Entities)
TUBE reagents (Hjerpe 2009) are recombinant fusions of four tandem UBA domains that bind
polyubiquitin chains with nanomolar affinity and out-compete DUBs. They enrich ubiquitinated
proteins from lysates for discovery proteomics. Non-quantitative relative to K-εGG but valuable
for orthogonal discovery , especially for detecting chain-type-specific substrates (UBA-UBQLN1,
NEMO-UBAN for linear/K63-biased).
Ubiquitin AQUA and Site-Specific Absolute Quantification
Kirkpatrick 2006 Nat Cell Biol and Phu 2011 MCP developed AQUA peptides with heavyisotope-labeled ubiquitin-linkage-specific tryptic peptides (LIFAGK(GG)QLEDGR for K48,
TLSDYNIQK(GG)ESTLHLVLR for K63, etc.). Spiked as internal standards, they give absolute
moles of each chain type. Relevant if the student wants to claim “DCAF7 builds K48 chains on
IFIT3.”
Approach

sections.title: Orthogonal Validation of a CRL-DCAF7 Substrate — the Burden of Proof

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
To claim that IFIT3 is a bona fide DCAF7-CRL4 substrate, the committee will expect the following
standard of evidence. This is distilled from gold-standard papers such as Mayor-Ruiz et al., 2020
Nat Chem Biol (DCAF15 as the aryl-sulfonamide substrate receptor for RBM39) and the broader
CRL substrate literature.
1. Co-IP demonstrating DCAF7-IFIT3 physical interaction. Endogenous co-IP preferred;
epitope-tagged reciprocal IPs (DCAF7-FLAG pulls IFIT3 and vice versa) are necessary .
Enhanced by MLN4924 or proteasome-inhibitor pre-treatment to trap the complex.
2. In vitro ubiquitination assay. Purified CRL4-DCAF7 (DDB1, CUL4A, RBX1, DCAF7) reconstituted with E1 (UBA1), an appropriate E2 (e.g., UBE2D3, UBE2R1/R2 for K48 chains), ubiquitin
and ATP , with purified IFIT3 as substrate. Detection is an SDS-PAGE/WB ladder of monoand
poly-Ub-IFIT3. Negative controls: omit E1, omit DCAF7, use an IFIT3 K-to-R mutant. This is
the definitive biochemical demonstration of direct, catalytic activity .
3. Cycloheximide chase (already in hand). IFIT3 half-life extended in DCAF7-KO HELs. This
is the student’s preliminary data and is the direct functional evidence of post-translational
stabilization.
4. Proteasome inhibitor rescue. MG132, bortezomib or carfilzomib should stabilize IFIT3 in
DCAF7-WT cells, confirming that DCAF7-mediated turnover is proteasomal rather than autophagic or lysosomal.
5. MLN4924 rescue. Pevonedistat should phenocopy DCAF7 loss by stabilizing IFIT3 — if it
does not, IFIT3 is not turned over by a cullin-RING ligase, and the hypothesis collapses.
6. K ￿ R mutagenesis. Mutation of the ubiquitination-site lysines identified in the K-εGG experiment (e.g., IFIT3-K-to-R at the mapped site) should render IFIT3 resistant to DCAF7-mediated
turnover. This is the direct test that the mapped sites are mechanistically necessary .
7. DCAF7 substrate-binding mutant. WDR-domain point mutations predicted to abrogate substrate recognition (guided by AlphaFold2-Multimer of DCAF7-IFIT3, or by structural homology to DDB1-DCAF complexes) should fail to degrade IFIT3 when re-expressed in DCAF7-KO
cells. This separates DCAF7 substrate receptor activity from DCAF7 scaffolding roles.

8. K-εGG site mapping on IFIT3. This is Aim 1b’s direct deliverable: under WT vs KO, which
IFIT3 lysines lose K-εGG signal? Those are the substrate sites.
The full package — physical interaction, in vitro activity , pharmacologic dependence (MG132 +
MLN4924), genetic necessity (KO + DCAF7 WDR mutant), site-level necessity (K-to-R) — is what
“nails down” a CRL substrate.

sections.title: Key Mass-Spec Concepts for Committee Q&A

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
This is a concept checklist; each item is fair game in oral questioning.
• FDR (False Discovery Rate): 1% at both peptide and protein level is standard. Calculated by
target-decoy searching — a reversed (or shuffled) version of the database is searched alongside
the real target; the ratio of decoy-to-target PSMs at a given score threshold estimates the false
discovery rate. Elias and Gygi 2007 Nat Methods is the reference.
• PEP (Posterior Error Probability): a per-PSM estimate of the probability that the match is
wrong given its score; more granular than FDR, which is a set-level property .
• Missed cleavages: trypsin cleaves C-terminal to K/R except before P . Standard searches allow
up to 2 missed cleavages. K-εGG peptides have an obligate missed cleavage at the modified
lysine because the GG remnant blocks tryptic access.
• Static vs dynamic modifications: static (fixed) modifications are assumed present on every instance of the residue (e.g., carbamidomethylation on Cys after IAA alkylation, +57.02 Da). Dynamic (variable) modifications are searched as optional at each site (e.g., Met oxidation +15.99,
K-GG +114.04).
• Fragmentation modes:
– HCD (higher-energy collisional dissociation): most common; robust b/y-ion spectra; compatible with PTMs including K-εGG.
– CID (collision-induced dissociation): older, low-mass cutoff, still used on ion traps.
– ETD (electron-transfer dissociation): cleaves N–Cα; preserves labile PTMs; works best on
high-charge precursors (3+ and above). EThcD adds supplemental HCD.
• Mass accuracy: Orbitrap routinely achieves <5 ppm; with lock-mass (internal calibration on
a polysiloxane standard), <1 ppm. Important because K-εGG (+114.04) must be distinguished
from other +114 mass shifts.
• Dynamic range: linear 3–4 orders of magnitude per run with DDA; compressed with TMT due
to co-isolation of interfering peptides in the MS2 isolation window (ratio compression, fixed by
SPS-MS3).
• Precursor isolation width: 0.7–1.2 Th typical; narrower reduces co-isolation.
• AGC target / injection time: controls ion flux to the Orbitrap; matters for quantitative precision.

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
Analysis is a multi-step pipeline: raw ￿ peptide IDs ￿ protein inference ￿ quantification ￿
statistics ￿ biological interpretation.
Stage Tool(s)
Raw search MaxQuant (Andromeda), FragPipe
(MSFragger), Proteome Discoverer (SEQUEST),
Spectronaut, DIA-NN
Statistical analysis Perseus, MSstats, limma, R/Python
Enrichment GSEA, g:Profiler, STRING, DA VID, Reactome,
EnrichR
PTM annotation PhosphoSitePlus, UbiBrowser, iPTMnet
Visualization Perseus, R (ggplot2, ComplexHeatmap),
Python (matplotlib, seaborn)
MaxQuant (Cox & Mann 2008 Nat Biotechnol), developed at Max Planck, remains the reference
tool for SILAC and LFQ. The student should plan to use MaxQuant for SILAC K-εGG and FragPipe
(MSFragger) for TMT K-εGG and DIA pilots.
Perseus is the downstream statistics and visualization partner to MaxQuant: volcano plots, t-tests
(Welch), 2D annotation enrichment, clustering, 1D Fisher tests.
PhosphoSitePlus curates known K-εGG sites from published datasets. The student should look
up IFIT3 on PhosphoSitePlus before the qual — any previously reported K-GG sites on IFIT3 are
prior knowledge and likely overlap with the sites the K-εGG experiment will identify . This is a
free, committee-safe hypothesis generator.
UbiBrowser and iPTMnet similarly aggregate E3-substrate predictions and annotations.
A Suggested Pipeline for Aim 1b K-εGG
1. 16-plex TMTpro: conditions = [DCAF7-WT vehicle, DCAF7-WT MLN4924, DCAF7-WT
MG132, DCAF7-KO vehicle, DCAF7-KO MLN4924, DCAF7-KO MG132] × biological
duplicates + bridge channels.
2. Acquire on Exploris 480 with SPS-MS3.
3. Search with FragPipe TMT-Integrator or MaxQuant; K-GG (+114.04) as variable modification;
1 missed cleavage at K-GG; 1% PSM and site FDR.
4. Quantify at site level; normalize to total protein abundance from the matched unenriched fraction (the “whole proteome” run from the same lysate).
5. Two-way contrast: KO-vs-WT and MLN4924-vs-vehicle.
6. “Real CRL substrate” call: site decreases in K-εGG upon DCAF7-KO AND protein increases
upon DCAF7-KO AND protein increases upon MLN4924.

sections.title: Ubiquitin and Ubiquitin-Like — the GG Remnant Problem (ISGylation!)

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
This is a biological-methodological hazard specifically salient to an IFN-centric thesis and the committee chair will almost certainly raise it.
The Problem
Modifier C-terminal sequence
Tryptic remnant on substrate
K
Ubiquitin …LRGG GG (+114.04)
NEDD8 …LRGG GG (+114.04) —
indistinguishable from Ub
ISG15 …LRLRGG GG (+114.04) —
indistinguishable from Ub
under standard trypsin
Standard K-εGG IP and MS cannot distinguish ubiquitination, neddylation, or ISGylation at the
level of the modified substrate lysine. All three leave an identical +114 GG remnant after trypsin.
Why This Matters for IFIT3 and DCAF7
IFIT3 is an interferon-stimulated gene (ISG). HERC5 is the major E3 ISG15 ligase and cotranslationally ISGylates newly synthesized ISGs on the ribosome. IFIT3 has been reported
to be ISGylated in IFN-stimulated contexts. If the K-εGG experiment is performed in IFN-α/βtreated, STAT1-active, ISG-high HELs (or in JAK2-V617F MPN cells, which have chronic IFN tone),
the K-εGG signal on IFIT3 will include ubiquitin AND ISG15 AND possibly NEDD8 remnants,
all summed.
Controls to Deconvolve
• MLN4924 / NAE inhibitor pre-treatment: NAE also activates the NEDD8 E1 (UBA3-NAE1)
and is required for NEDD8 conjugation. MLN4924 eliminates NEDD8 but does not block ubiquitin or ISG15. A MLN4924-insensitive K-εGG signal is ubiquitin + ISG15; a MLN4924-sensitive
signal is NEDD8.
• ISG15 knockdown or ISG15 knockout: ISG15-KO HELs (or ISG15 siRNA) eliminate ISGylation. The residual K-εGG signal is ubiquitin + NEDD8. Combined with MLN4924, this isolates
ubiquitin.
• UBE1L knockdown: UBE1L is the ISG15 E1. Knocking it down is an alternative ISGylationspecific control.
• Alternative proteases: LysC (cleaves only after K, not R) leaves longer ISG15 and NEDD8 remnants (LRGG for NEDD8, LRLRGG for ISG15) that are distinguishable by mass from the GG
ubiquitin remnant in some workflows — but the anti-K-εGG antibody is specific for the dipeptide remnant and LysC digestion reduces IP efficiency , so this is rarely used alone.

• Use the anti-K-LRGG antibody (NEDD8/ISG15-capable): some laboratories pair the standard
K-εGG IP with the K-LRGG anti-Nedd8-specific IP as parallel enrichments.
Practical Recommendation for Aim 1b
Include a vehicle / MLN4924 / ISG15-KD 3-arm control structure in the primary K-εGG experiment. Any IFIT3 site that (i) decreases upon DCAF7-KO, (ii) is MLN4924-sensitive, and (iii) is
ISG15-KD-insensitive is a bona fide DCAF7-CRL ubiquitination site. This is the rigorous deconvolution the committee will expect.

sections.title: Cycloheximide Chase — the Student’s Preliminary Data

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
Principle
Cycloheximide (CHX) binds the 60S ribosomal subunit and blocks the translocation step of elongation, halting new protein synthesis. Subsequent decay of existing protein is then a direct readout
of degradation kinetics. The plot of normalized protein (by Western blot densitometry , fluorescent
signal, or multiplex Western) against time follows first-order exponential decay in most cases, from
which t1/2 is extracted.
Protocol
• CHX at 50–100 µg/mL in complete media.
• Timepoints typically 0, 1, 2, 4, 6, 8, 12 h.
• Harvest, lyse in SDS buffer, run SDS-PAGE, Western for protein of interest.
• Quantify by densitometry (e.g., LI-COR Odyssey fluorescent secondary) or ECL.
• Normalize to a stable loading control (β-actin, vinculin, GAPDH, or tubulin — tubulin preferred because very long t1/2).
Controls
• MG132 (10 µM) co-treatment in a parallel arm: if CHX-decay is proteasomal, MG132 will prevent decay .
• Loading control (β-actin, vinculin, tubulin).
• Parallel short-half-life positive control (p53, cyclin D1, Myc, or another ISG known to turn over
fast) to confirm CHX activity in the assay .
Interpretation in the Student’s Case
IFIT3 half-life is extended in DCAF7-KO relative to WT HELs — this is direct, orthogonal evidence
of post-translational stabilization beyond the SILAC proteomics. Combined with the global MS hit,
the CHX chase is the strongest current preliminary datum for Aim 1b and will be front-and-center
in the committee’s view.

Limitations
• CHX itself triggers integrated stress response and, after ~8 h, apoptotic signaling — long chases
muddy the interpretation.
• CHX blocks new synthesis, including of the degradation machinery itself, so very long chases
can indirectly stabilize substrates.
• CHX chase is semi-quantitative (densitometry dynamic range ~1.5 logs); for rigorous t1/2,
pulse-SILAC is preferred.

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
The student should be able to at least name and briefly distinguish these if asked to compare against
CHX chase.
• Ribosome profiling (Ribo-Seq) — sequencing of ribosome-protected mRNA fragments, gives
translational rate. Combined with RNA-seq and proteomics, rules out the trivial explanation
that IFIT3 accumulates in DCAF7-KO because of increased transcription or translation.
• Click-iT OP-Puro / AHA / HPG — puromycin or methionine analogs with alkyne handles
that are click-conjugated to fluorophore or biotin post hoc; measure nascent protein synthesis
globally or per-protein.
• Pulse-chase with 35S-Met — classic; radioactive; gold standard for half-life before SILAC.
• Photoconvertible fluorescent proteins (Dendra2, PA-GFP , mEos3): tag-protein fusions photoconverted at t=0; decay of converted signal is live-cell t1/2. Most rigorous for single-protein
turnover kinetics but requires tagging and stable-cell-line construction.
• AI-driven degron prediction: recent work (Koren 2018 Cell on C-end rule, Timms 2019 Science
on N-degron grammar, Koren-Shental 2025 Nature on global degron grammar) proposes that
degron motifs can be predicted from sequence. For DCAF7-IFIT3, a predicted degron on IFIT3
(C-terminal or internal) would strengthen the hypothesis and could inform K-to-R mutagenesis
site selection.

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:

Reference List (Core Citations)
• Ong SE, Mann M. Nat Biotechnol 2002 — SILAC method paper.
• Xu G, Paige JS, Jaffrey SR. Nat Biotechnol 2010 — K-εGG diGly method (Bennett/Gygi companion: Kim 2011 Mol Cell).
• Kim W, Bennett EJ, Huttlin EL et al. Mol Cell 2011 — 19,000-site diGly proteome (Finley/Gygi).
• Buckley SM, Aranda-Orgilles B, Strikoudis A et al. Cell Stem Cell 2012 — hematopoietic K-GG
proteomics paradigm.
• Emanuele MJ, Elia AEH, Xu Q et al. Cell 2011 — systematic CRL substrate profiling with
MLN4924 + TMT.
• Soucy TA, Smith PG, Milhollen MA et al. Nature 2009 — MLN4924/pevonedistat cancer rationale.
• Ting L, Rad R, Gygi SP , Haas W. Nat Methods 2011 — TMT-SPS-MS3.
• Nabet B, Roberts JM, Buckley DL et al. Nat Chem Biol 2018 — dTAG system.
• Gao J, Buckley SM, Cimmino L et al. eLife 2015 — DCAF7 and DDB1-CUL4 placement (relevant
DCAF biology).
• Mayor-Ruiz C, Bauer S, Brand M et al. Nat Chem Biol 2020 — DCAF15-RBM39 paradigm for
CRL substrate identification.
• Elias JE, Gygi SP . Nat Methods 2007 — target-decoy FDR.
• Cox J, Mann M. Nat Biotechnol 2008 — MaxQuant.
• Schwanhäusser B, Busse D, Li N et al. Nature 2011 — pulsed-SILAC global half-lives.
• Kirkpatrick DS, Hathaway NA, Hanna J et al. Nat Cell Biol 2006 — Ub chain AQUA.
• Koren I, Timms RT, Kula T, Xu Q, Li MZ, Elledge SJ. Cell 2018; Timms RT et al. Science 2019 —
degron grammar.

Closing Note to the Student
The Aim 1b K-εGG experiment is the technical centerpiece of the thesis and the committee’s expectations are well-defined by the literature summarized above. The two most important things to
internalize before the oral are (i) the ISGylation confound and the explicit controls that deconvolve
it, because the committee chair’s combined UPS + IFN expertise will home in directly on this, and
(ii) the full orthogonal validation package for calling IFIT3 a bona fide CRL4-DCAF7 substrate,
because that is the standard set by papers like Mayor-Ruiz 2020 and is the burden of proof the
thesis must clear. Everything else — the instrumentation, acquisition modes, analysis pipelines —
is supporting infrastructure. Own the diGly method, own the ISGylation control logic, own the
validation hierarchy , and the rest of Section M follows.
Alternative Experimental Approaches

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
One of the most reliable features of a PhD qualifying exam is that the committee will ask some
version of the question, “What would you do if that fails?” In informal surveys of committee chairs,
this is the second-most-common line of questioning after the interpretive question “What would
this result mean?” Students often under-prepare for it because the primary experimental plan
feels, by the time of the exam, like a stable object: it has been written, rewritten, and defended in
lab meeting. Alternatives feel like a concession — an admission that the plan might not work. This
is the wrong framing. Alternatives are the explicit representation of a student’s understanding of
the failure modes of their own experiments, and committees read them as evidence of experimental
maturity .
For this proposal, the alternatives fall into three analytic categories:
1. T echnical failure — the assay itself does not work. Recombination is incomplete, the co-IP
produces no signal, the ubiquitinomics pipeline cannot resolve the modification stoichiometry , the iPSC organoid does not engraft. These are usually the easiest to address because the
fallbacks are often other established assays for the same biological question.
2. Biological failure — the assay works but the data do not support the hypothesis. DCAF7 loss
does not ameliorate MPN, the IFN signature is absent in scRNA-seq, the K-to-R mutant rescues
at an unexpected site. These require the student to think interpretively: is the hypothesis
wrong, is the readout wrong, or is the timing wrong?
3. Scope overshoot — the project succeeds too much and becomes larger than a dissertation can
contain. Aim 2 expands into an independent developmental biology program; the biochemistry in Aim 1b uncovers a structural story that merits a separate structural biology thesis. The
solution here is discipline rather than contingency .
The section below walks through each aim’s primary approach and specifies the decision logic
for moving to each fallback, along with the experimental signatures that should trigger a pivot.
Throughout, the guiding principle is the one that should be stated explicitly if a committee member

presses: a null result in one arm does not invalidate the thesis if there are converging lines of
evidence — genetic, pharmacologic, and biochemical — supporting the central model.

sections.title: Aim 1a Contingencies: The Mouse Model Arm

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
Aim 1a uses Jak2V617F; Rosa26-CreER; Dcaf7^fl/fl mice with tamoxifen induction and couples this
in vivo work to an iPSC bone marrow organoid system engrafted with CD34+ HSPCs from MPN
patients and edited by Cas9-RNP electroporation. Four distinct classes of technical failure are
foreseeable.

subsections.title: If Rosa26-CreER tamoxifen induction is inefficient or toxic
subsections.body:
Diagnostic signatures. The simplest indicator is a persistent floxed band on genomic PCR from
sorted HSCs or whole bone marrow after the standard five-day tamoxifen induction. A more subtle
symptom is a phenotype that appears gradual rather than binary between controls and induced animals — this usually indicates mosaic recombination, where a subset of stem cells escape excision
and outcompete excised clones over time. A third sign, more common with high-dose intraperitoneal tamoxifen in older mice, is acute GI toxicity or weight loss that confounds hematologic
endpoints.
Fallback options, ranked by ease of implementation:
Alternative Rationale Trade-offs
Lower-dose repeated
tamoxifen (1 mg/day for 10
days vs 4 mg/day for 5)
Reduces acute toxicity while
maintaining cumulative dose
Longer phenotype window;
may blur acute vs chronic
distinction
4-hydroxytamoxifen (4-OHT)
IP
Bypasses hepatic CYP
metabolism, more efficient
induction in young mice
More expensive; shorter
half-life, may need more
frequent dosing
Tamoxifen-containing chow
(e.g., Envigo TD.130856)
Chronic low-dose induction;
reduces injection stress
Slower kinetics; not suitable
for acute-induction
experiments
Scl-CreER (Gothert 2005) HSC-restricted expression;
cleaner lineage interpretation
Less efficient in MPP
compartment; slower kinetics
Fgd5-CreER (Gazit 2014) True long-term HSC marker Lower cell numbers for
downstream assays
Fallback experiment. If the issue is systemic tamoxifen toxicity rather than recombination inefficiency , switch to a competitive bone marrow transplant design: pretreat donor mice with tamoxifen, confirm excision in sorted donor HSCs, then transplant CD45.2 Jak2V617F; Dcaf7^Δ/Δ BM
with CD45.1 competitor BM into lethally irradiated CD45.1/.2 recipients. This separates the cellintrinsic phenotype from any systemic effect of tamoxifen, and the Cre+ and Crearms can be
tracked as separate engraftment curves.

subsections.title: If Jak2V617F; Dcaf7fl/fl double conditional has unexpected lethality
subsections.body:
Preliminary data from our lab and from the shared MPN consortium indicate that DCAF7 loss in
Jak2V617F context reduces disease severity — it is the therapeutic direction. Excess morbidity is
therefore unlikely . The relevant contingency is the ironic opposite finding, in which DCAF7 loss
worsens disease. This is not just a technical concern; it would require a real reinterpretation.
The first pivot is temporal granularity . The phenotype of Jak2V617F; Dcaf7^Δ/Δ at one week posttamoxifen may differ qualitatively from the phenotype at eight weeks: early DCAF7 loss may
permit a transient IFN-mediated clearance of Jak2V617F clones that is then followed by compensatory expansion of an IFN-refractory subclone. A two-timepoint analysis (acute one-week flow
+ CBC; chronic eight-week flow + histopathology + scRNA-seq) disambiguates “DCAF7 protects
against MPN” from “DCAF7 loss delays but does not prevent disease progression.”
The second pivot addresses germline confounds. A germline Jak2V617F allele produces systemic
effects — elevated EPO consumption, altered niche signaling, possible splenic sequestration — that
can interact with Dcaf7 loss in non-HSC-intrinsic ways. To remove this, use a transplant-based
system: lethally irradiate wild-type CD45.1 recipients, transplant Jak2V617F; Dcaf7^fl/fl; Rosa26CreER BM (CD45.2) with or without competitor, and induce with tamoxifen in the recipient. This
restricts Dcaf7 loss to the hematopoietic compartment and avoids systemic Jak2V617F effects from
embryonic onset.

subsections.title: If scRNA-seq does not reveal
subsections.body:
the expected IFN signature

This is a biological-failure contingency , and committees will probe it because the IFN signature is
the load-bearing inference connecting Aim 1a to Aim 1b.
First, rule out technical causes. Standard pipeline QC: mitochondrial percentage per cell (>15%
suggests stressed or dying cells), doublet rate (Scrublet or DoubletFinder; >8% is concerning), ambient RNA contamination (SoupX or CellBender), and sequencing saturation per cell. Low cell
number per arm (<2,000 cells per genotype for HSC/MPP compartments) produces noisy cluster
assignments and can obscure signatures that would be resolvable with deeper sampling. Clustering resolution matters disproportionately for the HSC/MPP axis, where over-clustering fragments
biologically meaningful populations.
Second, consider that the IFN signature may be transient or clone-specific. If the acute oneweek post-induction timepoint captures the IFN burst and the eight-week timepoint captures postadaptation steady state, then a single timepoint will miss the signal. The experimental response is
a kinetic scRNA-seq design (days 3, 7, 14, 28) with matched controls at each.
Third, switch modalities. If single-cell GEX is insufficient, the following orthogonal approaches
apply:
• CITE-seq with a focused antibody panel (CD150, CD48, CD34, Sca-1, Kit, Flt3, IFNAR1, IFNAR2, CD117, CD201) couples surface-marker immunophenotype to transcriptome and improves HSC identification.
• Multiome (scATAC + scRNA on the same nucleus) adds regulatory state information — an IFN
signature that is chromatin-primed but not transcribed would otherwise be invisible.
• Spatial transcriptomics (10X Visium or Xenium) resolves niche-level IFN signatures. If the
signature is localized to the endosteal region or to a specific macrophage-HSC interface, bulk

or dissociated approaches will dilute it beyond detection.
• Bulk RNA-seq on sorted populations (LT-HSC, ST-HSC, MPP2, MPP3/4, CMP , GMP , MEP)
is the highest-sensitivity orthogonal method if cell numbers allow. The trade-off is loss of intrapopulation heterogeneity .

subsections.title: If iPSC bone marrow organoids fail to engraft patient CD34+
subsections.body:
Patient-derived CD34+ engraftment into iPSC-derived bone marrow organoids is powerful but
notoriously variable. Published engraftment rates range from 20% to 70% depending on patient
sample, passage, and organoid batch. A failure here would not invalidate Aim 1a, because the in
vivo mouse arm carries the primary hypothesis, but it removes the critical human-cell validation.
Ranked alternatives:
1. OP9-DL4 co-culture — well-established monolayer, supports T and myeloid output, simple,
reproducible. Does not recapitulate niche geometry but is a stable platform for DCAF7 CRISPR
edits on patient CD34+.
2. MS-5 stromal co-culture — supports long-term culture-initiating cells (LTC-IC), more
myeloid-biased than OP9-DL4.
3. Primary autologous MSC co-culture — MSCs from the same patient BM aspirate provide a
more physiological niche, though cell number is limiting.
4. 3D hydrogel niches (Khan 2023 Nat Methods) — defined-matrix 3D systems without the
stroma variability of iPSC organoids.
5. NSGS or MISTRG xenograft — gold-standard functional readout for long-term HSC activity but expensive, time-consuming, and dependent on stable IACUC protocols. MISTRG has
better myeloid engraftment for MPN work.
Readout alternatives. Regardless of the culture format, the LTC-IC (long-term culture-initiating
cell) assay provides a functional readout of stem cell activity independent of morphological engraftment. For MPN-specific readouts, serial CFU replating and colony morphology scoring over
4-6 weeks remain standard.

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
Aim 1b defines the biochemical mechanism of DCAF7 on IFIT3 through K-εGG ubiquitinomics (with and without MLN4924), site-directed mutagenesis, and rescue. Each step has
well-characterized failure modes.

subsections.title: If K-εGG ubiquitinomics does not detect IFIT3 ubiquitination sites
subsections.body:
The most common reason for a negative result is low endogenous abundance: IFIT3 ubiquitination
at steady state in resting cells may be below the PTMScan K-εGG antibody’s practical detection
limit (~1 fmol per peptide on a typical Orbitrap run).
Workflow escalation:

Step Rationale
IFN-α pre-treatment (1000 U/mL, 6 h) Induces IFIT3 expression 10-50x, increases
substrate pool
MG132 (10 μM, 4 h) Blocks proteasome, accumulates ubiquitinated
species
Dcaf7 KO vs WT comparison Differential signal with KO serving as negative
reference
Targeted PRM for predicted IFIT3 peptides Switches from discovery to targeted
quantification, 10-100x sensitivity gain
TUBE (tandem ubiquitin-binding entity)
enrichment
Non-site-specific but captures
polyubiquitinated species
HA-Ub overexpression + His-Ub dual affinity Amplifies signal, known artifact risk
If none of the above produce signal, consider that IFIT3 may not be directly ubiquitinated —
DCAF7 may regulate IFIT3 via an adapter protein whose ubiquitination is the actual molecular
event. In that case, the biochemistry shifts to IP-MS on DCAF7 and IFIT3 separately and overlap
analysis.

subsections.title: If co-IP fails — no DCAF7-IFIT3 interaction detected
subsections.body:
CRL substrate interactions are often transient and short-lived, and standard co-IP may fail even
when the enzymatic relationship is real.
Fallbacks:
• Chemical crosslinking — DSS (disuccinimidyl suberate, membrane-permeable) or DSP (reversible) before lysis preserves transient complexes. This should be the first step.
• Proximity labeling with T urboID (Branon 2018 Nat Biotechnol) — fuse TurboID to the N- or
C-terminus of DCAF7; treat cells with 500 μM biotin for 10 minutes; streptavidin enrichment
of biotinylated proteins followed by MS. TurboID captures proximal proteins (~10 nm radius)
including transient ones. Shorter-label variants (BioID2, miniTurbo) trade sensitivity for temporal resolution.
• DDB1 as scaffolding IP — DCAF7 binds DDB1 within the CRL4 complex; IP of DDB1 followed
by IFIT3 western may reveal indirect association even when DCAF7-IFIT3 direct IP fails.
• In vitro binding — purified DCAF7-DDB1 complex (co-expressed in Hi5 or Sf9 insect cells) +
purified IFIT3 (E. coli if solubility permits, otherwise insect cell) in a pulldown. Biochemically
defined, removes cellular confounds.

subsections.title: If the in vitro ubiquitination assay fails
subsections.body:
In vitro reconstitution of CRL4 activity is one of the harder biochemistry assays, and first-attempt
failure is normal.
Checklist:

• E2 selection — CRLs prefer CDC34 or UBE2R1; UBE2D family (D1, D2, D3) also works for
initial priming. Missing E2 activity is the most common cause of no signal.
• Neddylation — Cul4A must be neddylated to be active. Options: (1) co-express with NEDD8
E1/E2/E3 in the reaction; (2) use commercially pre-neddylated Cul4A (BostonBiochem, R&D
Systems); (3) add NAE/UBE2M/RBX1 purified components.
• ATP regeneration — phosphocreatine/creatine kinase system for long reactions.
• Ubiquitin concentration — at least 10 μM in the reaction.
• Detergent/buffer — 0.01% Triton or similar to prevent precipitation of IFIT3.
If in vitro reconstitution remains refractory , the fallback is in-cell ubiquitination: co-transfect
HEK293T with Flag-DCAF7, HA-IFIT3, and His-Ub; denature with 6M guanidine-HCl; Ni-NTA
purify under denaturing conditions; blot HA. This loses kinetic control but is highly sensitive for
detecting ubiquitinated substrate.

subsections.title: If K-to-R mutagenesis rescues IFIT3 but at an unexpected site
subsections.body:
This is actually the expected outcome for most ubiquitination targets — the initial top-ranked lysine
often is not the regulatory site. The experimental response is iterative.
1. Collapse all IFIT3 lysines into a map, annotated with K-εGG peptide coverage, evolutionary
conservation (ConSurf), and surface accessibility (AlphaFold-derived SASA).
2. Prioritize by a weighted score — coverage × conservation × accessibility .
3. Build multi-lysine mutants (K-R pairs, triplets) to account for redundancy .
4. Consider non-lysine ubiquitination — N-terminal methionine ubiquitination (via Ub-M peptide bond) and Ser/Thr ester-linked ubiquitination (McClellan 2019 J Biol Chem) are rare but
documented, and would be missed by canonical K-εGG enrichment. Hydroxylamine sensitivity distinguishes ester from amide linkages.

subsections.title: If DCAF7 inhibition stabilizes IFIT3 but does not synergize with IFN-α
subsections.body:
A biological-failure contingency . The mechanistic story survives — DCAF7 does regulate IFIT3 —
but the therapeutic-combination hypothesis needs reassessment.
• Dose-response adjustment — BC18630 + IFN-α combination matrices (8×8 dose grid) to identify synergy windows. Loewe, HSA, and Bliss models may disagree.
• Cell line heterogeneity — HEL, SET-2, UKE-1 vary in STAT1/STAT5 ratios, JAK2 allele burden,
and IFN responsiveness. SET-2 is often a cleaner MPN model than HEL.
• Primary MPN samples — the definitive context. Cell-line synergy that does not translate to
primary is more common than the reverse.
• Genetic context tuning — because synergy may depend on STAT1/STAT5 balance, use
CRISPR-modulated STAT5 knockdown to test whether shifting the ratio rescues synergy .

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
Aim 2 is structurally higher-risk than Aim 1 because it relies on inferences about developmental
timing that cannot be fully established before the work begins.

subsections.title: If Vav-Cre phenotype is too severe for mechanistic follow-up
subsections.body:
Vav-iCre drives recombination from approximately E11.5 in hematopoietic precursors, and our
preliminary data indicate a P4 phenotype (reduced BM cellularity , anemia, perinatal morbidity).
Some Vav-iCre; Dcaf7^fl/fl pups fail to survive to weaning, which constrains downstream analyses.
Tiered alternatives:
Strategy Implementation Information yield
Foster-mother rescue Cross-foster at P0 to lactating
wild-type dams
Extends survival window;
standard for perinatal
phenotypes
Heterozygous Vav-iCre
Dcaf7^fl/+
Halves gene dosage Survivable if haploinsufficient;
tests dosage
Scl-CreER tamoxifen at P0/P4 Induced HSC-restricted KO at
precise timepoint
Disentangles onset of Dcaf7
requirement
CSF1R-iCre Later-onset, tissue
macrophage-biased
Tests whether phenotype is
macrophage- vs HSC-driven
Adult BMT + Dcaf7 KO
induction
Transplant fl/fl BM into
lethally irradiated recipients,
induce in adult recipient
Removes perinatal window
entirely; tests if adult HSC is
sensitive
The last option is particularly valuable: if adult HSCs after BMT + tamoxifen show no phenotype,
this supports a perinatal-specific requirement. If they show the same phenotype, DCAF7 is broadly
essential and the developmental framing is reinterpreted.

subsections.title: If P0-P14 flow does not resolve DCAF7 substrates in neonatal HSPCs
subsections.body:
Neonatal HSPC numbers are limiting (typically 10 3-104 LSK per pup at P4), and standard proteomics workflows require 10 5-106 cells. Several low-cell-number approaches exist.
• nanoPOTS-scale proteomics (Kelly 2020 Angew Chem) — enables proteomics from 100-1000
cells; collaboration or contract required.
• SCoPE-MS (Specht 2021 Nature) — single-cell proteomics via TMT multiplexing and carrierchannel amplification. ~1000 proteins per cell.
• Whole-BM proteomics at P4 — sacrifice population-level precision for depth; complements
scRNA-seq.
• Functional surrogate: perinatal IFN pulse measurement — rather than proteomics, measure
ISG expression (Ifit1, Ifit3, Isg15, Mx1) by RT-qPCR or RNAscope in Dcaf7 KO vs WT neonatal

BM at P4 and P7. If DCAF7 controls the termination of the perinatal IFN pulse, KO tissue will
show prolonged ISG expression.
• Retrospective tracing — deep scRNA-seq at P28 with lineage-tracing markers allows backinference of perinatal progenitor identity; useful when direct P4 proteomics is infeasible.

subsections.title: If DCAF7 is just globally essential, not specifically perinatal
subsections.body:
This is the most difficult alternative because it would reshape the thesis narrative. The central claim
of Aim 2 — that DCAF7 has a developmental-window-specific function — depends on DCAF7
being dispensable outside that window.
Disambiguation experiments:
1. E14.5 fetal liver hematopoiesis — Vav-iCre excises by E11.5, so fetal liver is already Dcaf7^Δ/Δ.
If fetal liver HSC numbers and CFU are normal, DCAF7 is dispensable at that stage.
2. Scl-CreER acute adult KO — induce in 8-week-old adults. If adult LT-HSC survive and maintain self-renewal, DCAF7 is dispensable in adult. If they crash, DCAF7 is essential broadly .
3. Combined: normal at E14.5 + normal in adult + failure at P4 ￿ true perinatal-window specificity . The alternative pattern — impaired at all stages but with P4 having the lowest functional reserve — would redirect the thesis toward a “stress-threshold” framing rather than a
“developmental-switch” framing.
Importantly , this pivot does not invalidate the MPN arm. If Aim 2 becomes a “DCAF7 is essential in hematopoiesis and the P4 phenotype reflects a demand-supply mismatch” story , Aim 1’s
therapeutic logic is unaffected — DCAF7 inhibition would still stabilize IFIT3 and synergize with
IFN-α in MPN.

subsections.title: If the perinatal IFN pulse (Li 2020) is not
subsections.body:
the relevant upstream signal

The Li 2020 Cell Stem Cell paper describes a physiological IFN burst at approximately P4-P14 in
mouse bone marrow that drives fetal-to-adult HSC transition. Our Aim 2 links DCAF7 to this
pulse. But the ISG signature at P4 could alternatively reflect commensal microbiota colonization
(which begins during this window and produces TLR-driven type I IFN) rather than the endogenous developmental program.
Discriminating experiments:
• Germ-free (GF) Dcaf7 V av-Cre — if the phenotype persists in GF animals, the driver is
microbiota-independent and the developmental-IFN framing holds. If it rescues, microbiotadriven IFN is the relevant signal.
• Ifnar1^-/- × Dcaf7 V av-Cre double — removes type I IFN signaling entirely . Rescue of the
hematopoietic phenotype in the double knockout would confirm that IFN (from either source)
is upstream. No rescue would force a search for a non-IFN upstream signal.
• Alternative substrate framing — if IFN is not the upstream signal, DCAF7 may act on substrates unrelated to the IFN pathway . HMGA2 (fetal HSC program) and SOX17 (fetal-to-adult
switch) are the leading alternative substrates; both have known E3 ligase regulation.

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
Aim 1c tests BC18630 + IFN-α synergy in Jak2V617F mice. The pharmacology is the weak link; the
biology is relatively robust.

subsections.title: If BC18630 is not bioavailable
subsections.body:
BC18630 bioavailability data in the Lear 2025 Sci Adv paper were based on in vitro and cell-line
studies; in vivo PK in immunocompetent MPN mouse models has not been fully characterized.
Plasma exposure may be insufficient for target engagement at tolerable doses.
Alternatives:
• BC18813 — structurally related DCAF7 inhibitor named in Lear 2025 with a different PK profile.
First fallback.
• dTAG-Dcaf7 (Nabet 2018 Nat Chem Biol) — knock-in dTAG into the endogenous Dcaf7 locus;
add dTAG-13 or dTAGv-1 for rapid chemical degradation in vivo. This converts the pharmacology problem into a genetics problem with drug-like temporal control.
• Dcaf7 shRNA or siRNA — inducible shRNA via doxycycline (tet-on). Pharmacologically imperfect but provides orthogonal in vivo validation.
• PROTAC/molecular glue versions of BC-series — theoretical but achievable in a lab with medchem capacity; partner with a core or collaborator.
• iPSC-MPN HSPC transplant + drug in recipient — if mouse PK is the blocker, shift to a
xenograft model where drug is delivered to an immunodeficient recipient.

subsections.title: If BC18630 has off-target effects
subsections.body:
Any small-molecule hit requires orthogonal genetic confirmation.
• Rosa26-CreER × Dcaf7^fl/fl + IFN-α — the genetic phenocopy of the pharmacologic arm. If
Dcaf7 KO + IFN-α produces the same synergy as BC18630 + IFN-α, the pharmacologic effect is
on-target. Divergence between the two indicates off-target contribution.
• Selectivity panels — KINOMEscan at the standard 10 μM, supplemented with a DCAFfocused panel (Lear 2025 reported partial profiling across ~15 DCAF family members).
Publicly available DCAF-selective ligand panels are expanding.
• Thermal proteome profiling (TPP) in MPN cells — unbiased assessment of direct binding partners of BC18630.

subsections.title: If the synergy is cell-autonomous in vitro but immune-mediated in vivo
subsections.body:
IFN-α has strong immune-modulatory effects, and a host-immune contribution to MPN clearance
is a well-documented confound.
• Immunodeficient BMT — transplant Jak2V617F BM into NSG or NRG recipients + BC18630
+ IFN-α. Loss of synergy in this context implicates host immunity; preservation implicates
cell-autonomous mechanism.

• In vitro cell-autonomous readouts — CFU assays, serial replating, competitive co-culture, cellline synergy (Bliss, Loewe). These cannot fully recapitulate in vivo biology but constrain the
cell-autonomous component.
• T-cell depletion cohort — anti-CD4 + anti-CD8 depletion in immunocompetent MPN mice ±
BC18630 + IFN-α dissects T-cell dependence specifically .

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
A qualifying committee will occasionally identify that the proposed work is too much. The failure
mode is not a technical one; it is the student spending four years producing partial results across
too many arms.
Three viable de-scope strategies:
1. Commit to Aim 1 as the thesis core; reposition Aim 2 as exploratory. The dissertation has a
single central claim (DCAF7 as MPN therapeutic target through IFIT3 stabilization) and Aim
2 appears as a final chapter of descriptive preliminary data setting up a postdoc or follow-on
project. This is the most defensible de-scope and is often the outcome even when not planned.
2. Add a co-author with developmental expertise for Aim 2. A senior lab member or collaborator in a developmental hematology group can take primary responsibility for the Vav-Cre and
perinatal arms, leaving the student to focus on the MPN and biochemistry arms. This requires
institutional flexibility and a good collaborator match.
3. Pre-register Aim 2 with go/no-go at 18 months. Write the Aim 2 hypothesis, primary outcome,
and decision criteria explicitly . If after 18 months the key disambiguation experiments (E14.5
vs adult, Ifnar1 cross, germ-free) have not produced supportive data, drop Aim 2 in favor of
deepening Aim 1b biochemistry .

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
Power planning is itself a contingency . Underpowered studies are one of the most common reasons
for “the assay worked but I can’t draw a conclusion” — the biological-failure category in disguise.

If effect sizes are smaller than assumed: a post-hoc power analysis on preliminary data updates
the sample size needed. Expand the follow-up cohort rather than draw conclusions from underpowered preliminary data. When this is infeasible, two strategies preserve interpretability: pooled
analysis across biological replicates (when technical variance dominates) and collaborative metaanalysis with independent cohorts.
Multiple testing. Benjamini-Hochberg FDR for all high-dimensional readouts (proteomics, RNAseq, ATAC). For targeted experiments with a pre-specified hypothesis (e.g., IFIT3 K63-linkage at
K228), Bonferroni is more appropriate. The a priori decision of “which peptides are primary hypotheses” must be made and documented before the data are unblinded.

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
This is not a separate contingency category — it is the background discipline against which all
contingencies are evaluated.
• Blinding. Flow analysis, pathology scoring, and CBC interpretation are performed with the
operator blinded to genotype. Mouse ear-tags are decoded only after analysis is complete.
• Sex as a biological variable. NIH mandate. Both sexes included in all mouse cohorts. Analysis
is powered to test sex × genotype interaction as a secondary analysis.
• Randomization. Treatment assignment within litters is randomized. For drug studies, randomization block design with matched littermates in each treatment arm.
• RRID citation. All cell lines (SET-2 RRID:CVCL_2187, HEL RRID:CVCL_0001, UKE-1
RRID:CVCL_A007, etc.), mice ( Jak2V617F JAX #031658, Dcaf7^fl generated in-house with
depositing plan), and key antibodies with validated clone and lot numbers.
• Pre-registration. Primary outcomes (e.g., “hematocrit at 8 weeks post-tamoxifen in Jak2V617F;
Dcaf7^Δ/Δ vs Jak2V617F; Dcaf7^+/+ ”) are registered on OSF or in an internal lab protocol
database before the first experiment. Secondary and exploratory analyses are clearly labeled
as such in the eventual manuscript.
• Raw data and code deposition. Raw fastq to GEO; proteomics raw to PRIDE; analysis code to
GitHub with a tagged release at the time of submission.

sections.title: Integration: Alternatives as Evidence of Maturity

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
A strong qualifying defense does not treat alternatives as concessions. The committee is not asking
“what will you do when your experiments fail” because they expect failure — they are asking because they want to hear the student articulate, in real time, the logic by which one moves between
experimental approaches when reality fails to conform to expectation. This is the essence of experimental maturity: the ability to predict failure modes before they occur, to recognize them when
they do, and to have a ranked list of next steps ready with pre-specified triggers for each.
The worst answer to “what if this fails” is “it won’t fail.” The second-worst is “I would think about
it when it happens.” The best answer — the one that ends the questioning in the student’s favor
— is a short list of two or three concrete alternatives, with diagnostic signatures that would trigger
each, and the logic by which those alternatives preserve or reinterpret the central thesis claim.
For this proposal, the structure of that answer is consistent across aims. The thesis hinges on
DCAF7 as a regulator of hematopoiesis in two contexts: MPN (Aim 1) and the fetal-to-adult transition (Aim 2). Each context has three independent lines of evidence — genetic, pharmacologic,
biochemical for Aim 1; temporal, substrate-level, and upstream-signal for Aim 2. The robustness
of the thesis comes from the triangulation of these independent lines. Any single technical or biological failure in one arm is addressable because the other arms continue to constrain the central
claim. The alternatives described in this section are not separate rescue plans; they are the reflection
of a modular experimental design in which the central claim is over-determined by the evidence.
When presenting alternatives in the defense, the recommended structure is: (1) state the primary
approach and the expected result, (2) name the one most likely failure mode and its diagnostic
signature, (3) state the ranked alternatives with the decision criterion that would trigger each, and
(4) close by stating how the thesis claim is preserved under each alternative. This four-part structure answers the committee’s question in full, shows the student has internalized the experimental
logic, and — critically — demonstrates that the student has thought about the project at a level of
granularity that matches the committee’s own expertise.
Key Citations Referenced in This Section
• Li Y , et al. 2020. Cell Stem Cell . Perinatal IFN pulse and fetal-to-adult HSC transition.
• Lear TB, et al. 2025. Science Advances . BC18630 and BC18813 DCAF7 inhibitors, selectivity
profiling.
• Branon TC, et al. 2018. Nature Biotechnology. TurboID proximity labeling.
• Nabet B, et al. 2018. Nature Chemical Biology . dTAG system for targeted protein degradation.
• Khan AO, et al. 2023. Nature Methods. 3D hydrogel niches for hematopoietic organoids.
• Specht H, et al. 2021. Nature (Genome Biology). SCoPE-MS single-cell proteomics.
• McClellan AJ, et al. 2019. J Biol Chem . Non-lysine ubiquitination mechanisms.
• Göthert JR, et al. 2005. Blood. Scl-CreER mouse line.
• Gazit R, et al. 2014. J Exp Med . Fgd5-CreER for HSC-specific labeling.
• Kelly RT, et al. 2020. Angew Chem. nanoPOTS low-input proteomics.

Integrative Methodological Synthesis
This final Tier 2 section ties the six methodology chapters (AML, CHIP/CH, Cre systems, flow
cytometry , ubiquitinomics, alternative approaches) back to the two aims. Use it as the spine for any
methodology-focused committee preamble and as a checklist of what must be rehearsed before the
exam. Unlike Tier 1’s integrative synthesis (Section H), which argued the biology, Section O argues
the experimental design: why each method was chosen, what it can and cannot prove, and how the
failures of one experiment are rescued by the others.

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
The experimental program rests on a three-tier evidence hierarchy : (1) a genetic tier that establishes necessity (Dcaf7-flox crossed to Rosa26-CreERT2 in adult JAK2V617F-Mullally mice, replacing the Poly(I:C)-confounded Mx1-Cre system), (2) a pharmacologic tier that establishes sufficiency
and therapeutic feasibility (BC18630 IC50 ~1.80 μM, Lear 2025 Sci Adv , combined with PEG-IFNα2a in MPLW515L or Jak2V617F BMT models), and (3) a biochemical tier that establishes mechanism (tag-switch K-εGG ubiquitinomics in DCAF7-KO versus WT HEL cells, paralleled by cycloheximide chase and SILAC protein-level mass spectrometry , layered against scRNA-seq of sorted
LSK/SLAM populations). The perinatal arm is a single genetic experiment (Vav-Cre Dcaf7 fl/fl
lethality at P4–P7) interrogated with perinatal LSK flow, serial sacrifice at E14.5/P0/P4/P7/P14,
and Ifnar1 rescue to determine whether the phenotype is IFN-dependent. Each tier is instrumented
with orthogonal validation: genetic ablation is cross-checked by pharmacology , pharmacology is
cross-checked by genetic ablation, and mechanism is cross-checked by site-directed mutagenesis
at predicted ubiquitination lysines on IFIT3. The design is engineered so that no single method’s
failure collapses the thesis — a core defense against “what if X fails” committee probes.

sections.title: Why this organization: rebutting “you’re throwing every method at it”

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
A committee that looks at Tier 2 as a whole will notice that the toolkit is large: CRISPR KO cell
lines, conditional mouse alleles, two Cre drivers, flow cytometry with 12+ color panels, SILAC,
TMT, K-εGG, scRNA-seq, CFU, BMT, PDX, organoid. The legitimate concern is methodological
tourism — using many techniques superficially rather than any one technique deeply . The rebuttal
has three parts:
1. Each method answers a different question that the others cannot. Ubiquitinomics identifies substrates; flow identifies which populations depend on DCAF7; scRNA-seq identifies
which transcriptional programs are disrupted; mouse genetics demonstrates in vivo necessity;
pharmacology demonstrates therapeutic tractability . Collapsing any arm leaves a gap in the
argument.
2. The methods cross-validate at predefined checkpoints. SILAC hits are re-validated by CHX
chase; CHX chase is re-validated by K-εGG; K-εGG is re-validated by in vitro ubiquitination; in

vitro ubiquitination is re-validated by K￿ R substrate mutagenesis. The genetic KO phenotype
is re-validated by pharmacologic BC18630 and by dTAG-DCAF7 acute degradation. scRNAseq phenotypes are re-validated by population-level RT-qPCR on flow-sorted LSK-SLAM.
3. The methods map to specific aims. Aim 1a uses the genetic and flow tiers; Aim 1b uses the
biochemical tier; Aim 2 uses Vav-Cre + perinatal flow + Ifnar1 rescue. Nothing is deployed for
its own sake. Every technique traces back to a sentence in the aims.

sections.title: The four methodological bridges (pair this with Section H’s three biological bridges)

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
Methodological Bridge 1 — How do you separate the DCAF7 effect from the Poly(I:C) effect in
Mx1-Cre? This is the single most likely methodological question and was raised explicitly in our
own Tier 1 Section H. The answer is fourfold: (a) we show matched Poly(I:C)-treated Cre-negative
controls whose ISG signature is already distinct from DCAF7-KO at d14 post-induction; (b) we
move to Rosa26-CreERT2 with tamoxifen induction for the definitive experiment in Aim 1a; (c) we
show dose-response with tamoxifen where DCAF7-KO severity tracks deletion efficiency , ruling
out a Cre-independent effect; (d) we repeat key findings with BC18630 pharmacologic inhibition
in wild-type Jak2V617F cells, where no Cre or Poly(I:C) is involved. This rescues all Mx1-Cre
preliminary data while moving the thesis onto a cleaner genetic backbone.
Methodological Bridge 2 — How do you prove DCAF7 directly ubiquitinates IFIT3, not indirectly stabilizes it via scaffolding DYRK1A/HIPK2? The burden of proof is standard: (a) K-εGG
in DCAF7-KO vs WT identifies the specific ubiquitination sites that disappear on IFIT3, (b) in vitro
ubiquitination with recombinant CRL4-DCAF7 on purified IFIT3 reconstitutes the modification, (c)
K￿ R mutagenesis at the identified lysines on IFIT3 blocks degradation and phenocopies DCAF7
loss, (d) a DCAF7 WD40 β-propeller mutant that cannot engage IFIT3 behaves like KO. Each experiment on its own has caveats; the four together are the field-standard burden of proof for any
CRL4-substrate pair (e.g., DCAF1-SAMHD1, DCAF15-RBM39). The DYRK/HIPK scaffolding role
is explicitly excluded by showing that DYRK1A/HIPK2 kinase activity inhibitors (harmine, TBID)
do not phenocopy DCAF7 loss for IFIT3 stabilization.
Methodological Bridge 3 — How do you prove MPN-selectivity with only one MPN model?
We use three orthogonal MPN systems: (a) Jak2V617F-Mullally conditional knock-in (the goldstandard PV/MF model), (b) MPLW515L retroviral BMT (ET/MF phenocopy), (c) patient-derived
xenografts from ruxolitinib-resistant MF samples. Selective dependency must be recapitulated
in at least two of three for the claim to stand. Parallel wild-type controls (normal HSCs from
age-matched C57BL/6 mice, non-transduced BMT recipients, healthy-donor CD34+) are run in
every experiment to show the null effect in the absence of JAK2V617F. If the pharmacologic arm
(BC18630) shows identical selectivity in all three models while sparing normal HSCs, the argument
is strong.
Methodological Bridge 4 — How do you distinguish cell-intrinsic from niche-driven effects
in the perinatal V av-Cre phenotype? Three layers of experiments: (a) chimeric WT + Vav-Cre
Dcaf7 fl/fl BM transplants show whether the defect is transferable by HSCs alone, (b) Ifnar1 cross
(Vav-Cre Dcaf7 fl/fl × Ifnar1 KO) separates IFN-driven lethality from IFN-independent defects, (c)
germ-free rederivation (or broad-spectrum antibiotics from E14.5 through weaning) tests whether
the perinatal IFN pulse described by Li 2020 — which has microbiota and fetal-skin components

— is the driving stimulus. If the phenotype survives Ifnar1 KO but not germ-free, the stimulus is
non-IFN inflammatory; if it survives germ-free but not Ifnar1 KO, it is specifically type I IFN; if it
survives both, DCAF7 has an IFN-independent role we have not yet characterized.

sections.title: Where the committee will push methodologically

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
Anticipate the following in roughly this order of likelihood:
• “Why Rosa26-CreERT2 and not V av-CreERT2?”Because the Vav-CreERT2 literature is sparse
compared with Rosa26-CreERT2 (V entura 2007 Nature), and because we want adult-onset, allcell tamoxifen-inducible deletion to separate the developmental Vav-Cre phenotype from the
adult MPN-relevant phenotype. Rosa26-CreERT2 is also better-characterized for tamoxifen
dose-titration protocols (Badea 2003) and has no hematopoietic-specific expression confounds.
• “What is your sample size calculation for the BMT experiments?” Based on Mullally et
al. 2010 Cancer Cell, a splenic weight effect of 1.8-fold with SD 0.4 requires n=8 per arm for
α=0.05 and β=0.2; we plan n=10 for 20% attrition buffer. Flow cytometric readouts of LSK expansion have tighter CV (~15%) and would require n=6 per arm; we use n=8 to harmonize arm
sizes.
• “How do you handle the CD45.1 vs CD45.2 fitness difference?” Mercier 2019 Cell Reports
flagged intrinsic LT-HSC fitness differences between CD45.1 and CD45.2 congenic backgrounds.
We use CD45.1/2 heterozygous recipients for competitive BMT (Jansson 2019 Exp Hematol
protocol) and/or pre-test with congenic-only 50:50 BMT to establish baseline chimerism ratios.
We do not use pure CD45.1 ￿ CD45.2 or vice versa for the primary readout.
• “K-εGG cannot distinguish ubiquitin from NEDD8 or ISG15. How do you handle that?”
Three controls: (a) parallel MS with NEDD8-KO and ISG15-KO cells (or pharmacologic
MLN4924 and USP18 knockdown arms), (b) anti-K48 and anti-K63 linkage-specific IPs on the
same samples (Komander & Rape 2012), (c) focused confirmation of IFIT3 ubiquitination by
ubiquitin KGG in vitro reconstitution with pure recombinant components. ISGylation is a
particularly acute concern in our system because IFIT3 itself is an ISG; the Koren 2018 / Timms
2019 degron-grammar controls are directly relevant.
• “Your 2D HEL cell work will not capture in vivo stem cell biology. What is your bridge?”
HEL is an instrumentation platform for ubiquitinomics; LSK-sorted primary mouse HSPCs
(sorted via MACSQuant Tyto for 10X-compatible viability) are the in vivo correlate. We plan
to repeat the top-5 K-εGG hits on LSK-sorted BM from Rosa26-CreERT2 Dcaf7 mice at d14
post-tamoxifen. This is the single most important orthogonal experiment.
• “Your dTAG system requires endogenous tagging at the Dcaf7 locus. How do you validate
the tag doesn’t disrupt function?” Three validations: (a) Western and MS to confirm tagged
protein levels match untagged, (b) co-IP of tagged DCAF7 with DDB1 and DYRK1A confirms
scaffolding interactions intact, (c) rescue of CRISPR-KO phenotype by re-expression of tagged
protein demonstrates functional equivalence.
• “What pre-registration or analysis-plan discipline do you have?” All primary outcomes
(molecular remission by JAK2V617F allele burden, splenic weight at 6-week endpoint, LSK

frequency at 4-week endpoint) and all ubiquitinomics hit-calling thresholds (≥2-fold change,
q < 0.05, >2 unique peptides, detected in ≥3 of 4 biological replicates) are pre-specified.
Secondary exploratory analyses are explicitly flagged as such.

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
A strong methodological defense concedes limits before the committee extracts them:
1. No CROP-seq. We have not planned a DCAF7-specific CRISPR screen in primary HSPCs to
identify co-dependencies. Lane 2019 Nature Methods protocol is accessible but out of scope
for this aim package. If the committee asks, acknowledge the gap and propose it as a future
direction.
2. No cryo-EM of CRL4-DCAF7-IFIT3. A structural understanding of how IFIT3 engages
DCAF7’s WD40 β-propeller is beyond thesis scope. AlphaFold3 predictions (proposed in
Aim 1b) are a computational surrogate but cannot substitute for experimental structure
determination.
3. No lineage tracing within the perinatal window. The Vav-Cre P4 phenotype would benefit from barcoding (e.g., LARRY-mCherry barcodes à la Weinreb 2020 Science) to track clonal
output through the fetal-to-adult transition. This is a logical extension but is not part of Aim
2.
4. No germline-relevant human genetics. We have no evidence that DCAF7 is a Mendelian
disease gene, and Sakaguchi 2013 does not list it in pediatric myeloid malignancy . The VavCre phenotype therefore does not currently have a human clinical correlate beyond the MPNdrug-target premise.
5. No ubiquitin-independent scaffolding controls. DCAF7 has a characterized scaffolding role
in DYRK/HIPK kinase complexes (Glenewinkel 2016 Sci Rep). We do not currently have a
DCAF7 allele that is selectively scaffolding-competent but ubiquitin-ligase-dead. Structureguided design of such a separation-of-function mutant is a proposed future direction.

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.body:
Aim 1a — Is DCAF7 required for MPN maintenance? - Genetic: Rosa26-CreERT2 Dcaf7 fl/fl ×
Jak2V617F-Mullally; tamoxifen at 4 weeks post-pIpC induction of JAK2V617F; 6-week endpoint
with CBC, splenic weight, BM cellularity , LSK/SLAM flow, JAK2V617F allele burden. - Pharmacologic: BC18630 monotherapy and combination with PEG-IFN-α2a in Jak2V617F-Mullally mice
at matched endpoints; IC50 and splenic reduction dose-response. - Flow: 12-color panel with
LSK, SLAM MPP1-4, myeloid differentiation (Gr1, Mac1, Ter119), megakaryocyte (CD41, CD42),
lymphoid (B220, CD3) readouts on BM, spleen, and peripheral blood. - Single-cell: scRNA-seq
(10X Chromium) on LSK-sorted BM from 3 arms (WT+tam, Jak2V617F+tam, Jak2V617F Dcaf7KO+tam), n=3 per arm with sample multiplexing.

Aim 1b — How does DCAF7 degrade IFIT3? - Substrate ID: K-εGG TMT-16plex on HEL DCAF7KO vs WT ± IFN-α (4 conditions × 4 replicates); parallel SILAC protein-level MS. - Direct validation:
CHX chase for IFIT3 and top-5 other hits; in vitro ubiquitination with recombinant CRL4-DCAF7 +
IFIT3; IFIT3 K￿ R lysine mutagenesis. - Scaffolding control: DYRK1A/HIPK2 inhibitors (harmine,
TBID) fail to phenocopy . - ISGylation control: NEDD8-KO, ISG15-KO, and MLN4924/USP18
knockdown parallel arms.
Aim 2 — Why does DCAF7 become essential at P4? - Genetic: Vav-Cre Dcaf7 fl/fl serial sacrifice
at E14.5, P0, P4, P7, P14; full survival cohort. - Rescue: Vav-Cre Dcaf7 fl/fl × Ifnar1 KO cross to test
IFN-dependence. - Environmental: Germ-free rederivation or E14.5-to-weaning broad-spectrum
antibiotics to test microbiota-driven IFN pulse component. - Cellular: Perinatal LSK flow, CD34±,
CD150/CD48 SLAM across the window; BM and fetal liver timepoints. - Biochemical: DCAF7 protein by Western and IFIT3 protein by Western on sorted perinatal LSK at each timepoint, paralleling
Blake MGD mRNA expression curve.
