Follow-ups have pushed edit multiplicity higher, added lineage-tracing barcodes, and extended the approach to murine HSCs with similar efficiencies.

For Huey, CRISPR-RNP in primary HSPCs offers a complementary tool to the Dcaf7^F/F^ conditional model: CRISPR-editing of DCAF7 (with or without additional JAK2V617F overexpression) in human CD34^+^ cells followed by NSG transplant gives a human-system readout of DCAF7 loss in the context of JAK2V617F, bridging species. More immediately, she can use the same approach to score DCAF7 as a dependency — sgRNA-RNP targeting DCAF7 in JAK2V617F-mutant human HSPCs — and to test whether IFIT3 knockout rescues the DCAF7-loss phenotype (a double-edit experiment straightforward to implement in the Tothova framework). The committee will expect her to know that RNP-based CRISPR in primary HSPCs is the current standard, that lentiviral Cas9 is inferior, and that 8-12 week NSG readouts are the benchmark for long-term repopulating activity.

Tothova Z, Krill-Burger JM, Popova KD, et al. Cell Stem Cell. 2017 Oct 5;21(4):547-555.e8. PMID: 28985529.

---

qanda.q: If you lose DCAF7, IFIT3 is stabilized — but isn't IFIT1 the effector that binds capped RNA? How does IFIT3 alone change antiviral output?
qanda.a: Integrate Johnson 2018 (IFIT3 C-terminal domain both stabilizes IFIT1 and allosterically enforces cap-0 specificity) with Fleith 2018 Tier 1 (heterotrimer is an order of magnitude more potent at translation inhibition than any monomer), and Terenzi 2006 (IFIT2 contributes an independent eIF3-c contact). The answer: stabilized IFIT3 means more and more-specific IFIT1 on-cap, plus a more stable heterotrimer, plus more IFIT2-dependent eIF3 dual inhibition.
qanda.section: §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Do you have an Ifit3-knockout mouse, and if not why not?
qanda.a: Invoke Pichlmair 2011 (Ifit1−/− exists), Fensterl 2012 (Ifit2−/− exists), and especially Johnson 2018, which showed that the C-terminal IFIT1-interacting domain of human IFIT3 is absent in mouse Ifit3. The implication is that a straight mouse Ifit3 knockout does not faithfully model human IFIT3 loss; humanization or cross-complementation would be required. Huey can instead argue for a conditional Dcaf7-deletion MPN model where she measures IFIT3 stabilization directly.
qanda.section: §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis

qanda.q: What RNA is the stabilized heterotrimer actually going to bind in your MPN cells?
qanda.a: Walk the committee through Pichlmair 2011 (PPP-RNA, Kd ~ nM, R187 in C-terminal groove), Daffis 2010 (cap-0 vs cap-1 host-self discrimination), Abbas 2013 (IFIT5 PPP cavity at atomic resolution), Abbas 2017 (IFIT1 cap-0 tunnel at 1.6 Å), and Katibah 2014 (IFIT5 also binds cellular pol III PPP transcripts and pre-tRNAs). The most likely pools in MPN are viral-like or hypomethylated self-RNAs upregulated under JAK2V617F stress.
qanda.section: §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Isn't IFIT3 anti-apoptotic? Why would stabilizing it help kill MPN clones?
qanda.a: Confront Hsu 2013 (in DV-infected A549, IFIT3 knockdown is pro-apoptotic; IFIT3 restrains caspases) and Stawowczyk 2011 (IFIT3 suppresses IFIT2-driven intrinsic apoptosis). Counter with Xiao 2006 (IFIT3/RIG-G drives G1/S arrest via p27 stabilization and c-myc/p21 in APL and U937 myeloid cells) and He 2024 (IFIT3 is required for GSDME-dependent pyroptosis in myeloma/leukemia). The net statement: IFIT3's anti-death vs pro-arrest vs pro-pyroptosis balance is cell-type-specific, and in JAK2V617F myeloid cells the dominant output is antiproliferative and IFN-amplifying.
qanda.section: §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis

qanda.q: What is the precedent for a cullin-RING E3 ligase targeting IFIT3?
qanda.a: Cite Liu 2019 (vaccinia C9 is an F-box/ankyrin-repeat bridge between IFIT1/2/3 and SCF-cullin-1-SKP1, directing proteasomal degradation) and Li 2023 Tier 2 (UBE2O ubiquitinates IFIT3 K236 in HCC). Both precedents establish that UPS-mediated IFIT3 turnover is a physiological (and pathological) control mechanism. The DCAF7→CRL4→IFIT3 axis fits this precedent cleanly.
qanda.section: §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis

qanda.q: How do you distinguish the IFIT3 amplifier function on MAVS from its effector function in the heterotrimer?
qanda.a: Cite Liu 2011 for the MAVS-TBK1 interface (IFIT3 E164/E165 contacts MAVS, IFIT3 N-terminus contacts TBK1 K38) and Johnson 2018 for the IFIT1-interacting C-terminal domain. These are physically separable surfaces. Huey can propose reconstitution of separation-of-function IFIT3 mutants (E164A/E165A vs a C-terminal-truncation) in IFIT3-null MPN cells to dissect amplifier vs heterotrimer function.
qanda.section: §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Your mouse model — how will you measure IFIT3-dependent IFN amplification in vivo?
qanda.a: Use Butchi 2013 as the template: sort macrophages/microglia (or in Huey's case, HSPCs, granulocyte-monocyte progenitors, and MPN megakaryocytic precursors), measure Ifna/Ifnb induction and downstream ISGs ex vivo, and demonstrate an amplifier-loss phenotype in DCAF7-deficient or -sufficient cells ± IFN-α challenge.
qanda.section: §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Is this axis druggable / therapeutically relevant?
qanda.a: Combine Liu 2019 (viruses degrade IFITs via SCF, suggesting pharmacologic CRL4-DCAF7 inhibition should stabilize IFIT3), Xiao 2006 (stabilized IFIT3 is antiproliferative via p21/p27 in myeloid cells), He 2024 (IFIT3 is required for GSDME pyroptosis in leukemia/myeloma and synergizes with venetoclax), and the Tier 1 Fleith 2018 and Chikhalya 2021 papers (heterotrimer and MAVS amplifier). The translational pitch is: DCAF7 inhibition + IFN-α is the mainstay, with venetoclax combination as a natural extension for stabilized-IFIT3-primed MPN clones. ---
qanda.section: §1 — IFIT Family: Deep Literature Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Has DCAF7 ever been shown to mediate proteasomal degradation of a defined substrate?
qanda.a: Yes, twice independently. Peng 2016 showed CRL4^DCAF7 ubiquitylates DNA ligase I in HEK293T cells with diGly site mapping, recombinant reconstitution, and MG132 rescue [Peng 2016]. Lear 2025 showed CRL4^DCAF7 ubiquitylates TFEB in BEAS-2B airway epithelial cells with CHX chase in DCAF7-knockout cells and full recombinant in vitro reconstitution, and generated a selective small-molecule inhibitor (BC18630) that disrupts the DCAF7–TFEB interaction [Lear 2025].
qanda.section: §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Is there any literature precedent for DCAF7 regulating the type-I interferon response?
qanda.a: Zemke & Berk 2017 showed that a DCAF7–FOXK–CtBP complex is subverted by adenovirus E1A to suppress a delayed wave of type-I ISGs in primary human airway epithelium, and that disruption of this complex causes ~10-fold protein-level stabilization of IRF3 without mRNA change, driving ISG induction including IFIT2, ISG15, and OASL [Zemke & Berk 2017]. Trilling 2011 showed that a hijacked CRL4–DDB1 complex degrades STAT2 during MCMV infection, the direct viral precedent for CRL4 targeting of an IFN effector [Trilling 2011].
qanda.section: §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: How do I prove a phenotype runs through the CRL4 pathway and not an orthogonal E3?
qanda.a: Standard orthogonal tests, all validated in the above literature: MG132 pharmacologic rescue (stabilizes all proteasomal substrates); MLN4924/pevonedistat NAE inhibition (selectively blocks all CRL-family output) [Soucy 2009]; DDB1 siRNA knockdown phenocopy [Trilling 2011]; in vitro reconstitution with recombinant CUL4A/NEDD8/RBX1, DDB1, DCAF7, E1, E2, ubiquitin [Peng 2016; Lear 2025]; hyponeddylation-rescue chemical screen as the gold-standard test for CRL-dependence [Mayor-Ruiz 2020].
qanda.section: §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: What is the structural logic by which DCAF7 docks into the CRL4 apparatus?
qanda.a: DCAF7 is a WD40 β-propeller protein; the DDB1 double-β-propeller presents the substrate-receptor platform onto which DCAF7 docks through WDXR-family motif contacts [Angers 2006; Jin 2006]. The same architectural surface binds CRBN [Fischer 2014], DCAF15, and other DCAFs. This is the validated substrate-receptor grammar.
qanda.section: §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: DCAF7 is a kinase scaffold in published literature — doesn't that rule out a ubiquitin-ligase role?
qanda.a: No. The WD40 propeller has distinct binding faces: DDB1 contacts the propeller surface, while kinases like DYRK1A, HIPK2, and MEKK1 bind through flanking residues [Miyata & Nishida 2011; Ritterhoff 2010]. Peng 2016 and Lear 2025 demonstrate both activities coexist and that CRL4^DCAF7 catalyzes substrate ubiquitylation in defined in vitro systems. The Melo-Cardenas data extend this coexistence to IFIT3 in JAK2V617F MPN.
qanda.section: §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Is DCAF7 essential — will we kill the mouse?
qanda.a: Yes, constitutive DCAF7 loss is embryonic-lethal with IUGR, HIF-1 pathway activation, and developmental gene deregulation [Wu 2025]. This is why conditional approaches (Vav-Cre, Mx1-Cre, inducible shRNA) are required for the hematopoietic analysis in JAK2V617F MPN.
qanda.section: §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Can you actually drug DCAF7 pharmacologically?
qanda.a: BC18630 and BC18813 are selective DCAF7 inhibitors developed by Lear 2025 that disrupt the DCAF7–substrate interface; the Melo-Cardenas Lab uses these compounds. The broader molecular-glue/IMiD literature [Fischer 2014; Petzold 2016; Matyskiela 2018; Słabicki 2020; Mayor-Ruiz 2020] provides an extensive medicinal-chemistry playbook for selectively modulating CRL4 substrate recruitment.
qanda.section: §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Can a single CRL4 complex really recognize structurally diverse substrates like IFIT3 in addition to its published clients?
qanda.a: Yes. CRBN alone targets IKZF1, IKZF3, CK1α, SALL4, MEIS2, and growing numbers of C2H2 zinc-finger neo-substrates [Fischer 2014; Petzold 2016; Matyskiela 2018], and CRL4B can be redirected to cyclin K in the complete absence of a dedicated receptor [Słabicki 2020; Mayor-Ruiz 2020]. The DDB1 platform is intrinsically flexible. For DCAF7 specifically, the validated repertoire (LIG1, TFEB, plus the DCAF7–FOXK–CtBP-managed IRF3/STAT pool) spans DNA replication, autophagy/lysosomal control, and IFN signaling — consistent with the proposal that IFIT3 is a regulated client in JAK2V617F cells.
qanda.section: §2 — DCAF7, CRL4, and the E3 Ligase Landscape: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Why Mullally 2010 and not Akada 2010?
qanda.a: Both are conditional Jak2V617F knock-ins with Cre-inducible expression from the endogenous promoter; the Mullally allele is what the Melo-Cardenas lab has crossed to DCAF7-floxed and MxCre lines. The Akada allele is a useful orthogonal validation and allows gene-dose (het vs hom) comparisons, which Mullally heterozygous-only data cannot address. A proper defense acknowledges Akada as a planned validation.
qanda.section: §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Which of the four 2005 papers would you cite as the discovery paper, and why do you know all four?
qanda.a: Conventional citation practice treats them as co-equal; the methodological differences (LOH-guided, functional, cohort-based, candidate-gene) matter because they collectively establish somatic status (Baxter/Kralovics/Levine germline controls), sufficiency in vivo (James BMT), HSC-level mutation (Baxter/Levine colony work), and clinical correlates (Kralovics).
qanda.section: §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: What does 9pLOH mean and why does it matter?
qanda.a: Uniparental disomy generated by mitotic recombination converts heterozygous V617F to homozygous V617F without altering copy number. It matters because it maps onto clinical severity (Kralovics 2005 cohort; Akada 2010 mouse gene-dose) and because it is the cytogenetic footprint that allowed Baxter/Kralovics to localise the driver.
qanda.section: §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Does DCAF7 loss phenocopy IFN-α? Distinguish your mechanism from being a simple IFN agonist.
qanda.a: Mullally 2013 and Hasan 2013 together define what pharmacological IFN-α does in the Jak2V617F model (drives mutant HSCs out of quiescence, depletes disease-initiating cells on secondary BMT, erythroid lineage bias). DCAF7 loss is predicted to phenocopy that outcome, but via cell-intrinsic IFIT3 stabilisation rather than systemic Type I IFN exposure — a feature if the goal is continuous HSC engagement (Williams 2022 argument), but a mechanism that must be formally dissected by IFN-α receptor (IFNAR1) co-deletion to rule out autocrine IFN as the true driver.
qanda.section: §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Why does ruxolitinib not cure MPN, and what does that teach you about where DCAF7 loss should act?
qanda.a: COMFORT-I demonstrated cytoreduction and symptom relief without molecular response; Mullally 2010 had already shown that JAK2 inhibition does not eliminate disease-initiating HSCs. Ruxolitinib hits JAK2 kinase activity but not the upstream pathway wiring or downstream survival signals at the HSC level. DCAF7 loss is hypothesised to act via amplified IFN/ISG output, which Mullally 2013 shows is active at the HSC level — a different (and complementary) mechanism to JAK2 kinase inhibition.
qanda.section: §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Does your mechanism extend to CALR-mutant MPN?
qanda.a: Elf 2016 and Marty 2016 establish that CALR-mutant MPN signals through MPL-JAK2-STAT5, sharing the downstream pathway with V617F. If DCAF7-IFIT3 acts on IFN signalling and JAK1 rather than specifically on V617F, the mechanism is predicted to generalise to CALR-mutant disease. An aim 3 or future direction would be CALR-knockin × DCAF7-flox.
qanda.section: §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: How do you think about MPN timing given Williams 2022 and Van Egeren 2021?
qanda.a: JAK2V617F is acquired in childhood (in utero to ~19 years) and MPN diagnosis follows by a mean of ~30 years. The therapeutic target is a long-lived HSC clone. This argues for a durable, cell-intrinsic intervention such as a genetic DCAF7 loss (or a selective small-molecule DCAF7 disruptor) over intermittent systemic IFN-α dosing.
qanda.section: §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: What is the evidence that V617F confers an HSC-intrinsic advantage?
qanda.a: Mullally 2010 murine BMT showed no selective advantage in competitive transplant, but Hasan 2013 argued for age-dependent expansion of LSK/SLAM compartments in the Villeval allele, and Van Egeren 2021 quantified a human HSC-level fitness advantage in situ. The reconciliation is that the mouse BMT readout under-reads the human decadal-timescale advantage.
qanda.section: §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Why do some patients respond to IFN-α and others do not, and could DCAF7 loss sensitise non-responders?
qanda.a: Hasan 2013 and the Kiladjian-era clinical data suggest baseline ISG signature correlates with IFN response. If DCAF7 loss elevates baseline IFIT3 (and ISG output), it could in principle convert non-responders to responders or reduce the required IFN-α dose. This is directly testable in the Mullally model.
qanda.section: §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Scott 2007 JAK2 exon 12 — is your mechanism relevant there?
qanda.a: Exon 12 mutations activate JAK2 via a different autoinhibition-release mechanism than V617F but funnel through the same JAK2-STAT signalling. IFN-α is active in exon-12 PV clinically. The DCAF7 mechanism, operating downstream of IFN receptor engagement, should be insensitive to the specific JAK2 activation mechanism — a predicted strength of the approach.
qanda.section: §3 — JAK2V617F and the Myeloproliferative Neoplasms: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Pichlmair 2011 asks what IFIT1 recognizes and what Ifit1-/- mice are susceptible to. Draw the experimental logic: what were the three viruses chosen, and why was influenza the negative control? What does this tell you about the 5′-ppp dependence of IFIT1 function, and by analogy, what specific RNA species would you predict IFIT3 engages?
qanda.section: §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: The βTrCP/SCF complex terminates IFNAR1 through a ubiquitin-primed endocytic motif (Kumar 2007). Describe the mechanism in molecular detail — priming kinase, recognition motif, polyubiquitin topology, endocytic signal. Now draw the parallel architecture for DCAF7/CUL4 acting on IFIT3: which steps are conserved, and which are not? Where would you place IFIT3 polyubiquitination in this scheme?
qanda.section: §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: ISG15 deficiency in humans (Bogunovic 2012, Zhang 2015) produces paradoxical hyper-IFN because ISG15 stabilizes USP18. How does this precedent inform the interpretation of DCAF7 loss producing elevated IFIT3 and amplified IFN-α in MPN? Is DCAF7-IFIT3 mechanistically analogous (a UPS factor that normally dampens IFN) or mechanistically inverse? Justify.
qanda.section: §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Bowie 2007 defines the fetal-to-adult HSC switch at 3–4 weeks postnatally; Copley 2013 shows Lin28b-let-7-Hmga2 drives the fetal program. Your Axis 2 DCAF7 requirement is at P4. Does your current data support DCAF7 acting (a) within the fetal program as a Lin28b co-factor, (b) as a driver of the fetal-to-adult switch, or (c) independent of the Lin28b axis? What single qPCR panel on DCAF7-KO P4 HSCs would discriminate these?
qanda.section: §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Harding 2017 and Mackenzie 2017 establish that micronuclei from replication stress and mitotic errors trigger cGAS-STING and chronic IFN. JAK2V617F drives replication stress. How would you test whether the elevated IFN signature in JAK2V617F MPN is cGAS-STING-dependent, and whether DCAF7-loss amplification of IFIT3 acts upstream or downstream of this trigger? Name the pharmacologic tools (cGAS inhibitor, STING inhibitor) and the epistasis experiment.
qanda.section: §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Walter 2015 argues that IFN-driven dormancy exit produces DNA-damage-induced HSC attrition; Wilson 2015 shows this attrition preferentially affects the deeply quiescent subset at single-cell resolution. In your DCAF7-loss + JAK2V617F model, predict the HSC compartment phenotype at single-cell resolution, and what serial transplantation at limiting dilution should reveal after 16 weeks. What is your negative-control arm (DCAF7-loss in JAK2-WT)?
qanda.section: §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Stark & Darnell 2012 emphasize that JAK-STAT output is set by the balance between ligand-receptor activation (positive) and SOCS/USP18/IFNAR1-turnover (negative). Where does the DCAF7-IFIT3 axis sit in this regulatory hierarchy? Is it receptor-proximal, transcription-factor-level, or post-translational/effector-level? Why is this distinction important for the therapeutic implication of DCAF7 inhibition?
qanda.section: §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: Crow 2006 established AGS (TREX1 loss) as the prototypical type I interferonopathy — a Mendelian disease of mishandled self-nucleic-acid. Frame JAK2V617F MPN as a "somatic interferonopathy." What is the analogous somatic driver of cytosolic nucleic-acid accumulation, what is the analogous upstream sensor, and what is the therapeutic implication — specifically, does ruxolitinib address the sensor or the downstream signaling, and where would DCAF7 inhibition fit in this causal chain?
qanda.section: §4 — Interferon Signaling, ISGs, and Hematopoiesis: Deep Gap Analysis

qanda.q: You chose Mx1-Cre rather than Vav-iCre for conditional Dcaf7 deletion. Walk us through why — and what confound does poly(I:C) introduce in a study whose outcome measure is IFN signaling?
qanda.a: (Refs: Kühn 1995; de Boer 2003.) Expected answer: developmental essentiality concerns drive the adult-inducible choice; poly(I:C) triggers type I IFN signaling via TLR3/MDA5, which itself perturbs the DCAF7-IFIT3-IFN axis; the mitigation is a 3-4 week recovery period before phenotyping, plus orthogonal Rosa26-CreERT2 validation.

qanda.q: In your SLAM gating, what fraction of CD150^+^CD48^−^ LSK cells gives long-term multilineage reconstitution in limiting-dilution BMT, and why does this quantitative benchmark matter for interpreting your DCAF7 loss phenotype?
qanda.a: (Ref: Kiel 2005.) Expected answer: ~47% from the original Kiel paper; this purity matters because the observed LT-HSC expansion in JAK2V617F or contraction on Dcaf7 loss is masked in the heterogeneous LSK gate.

qanda.q: Pevonedistat inhibits NAE and blocks cullin neddylation. Why does this specifically implicate a cullin-RING ligase in IFIT3 turnover rather than simply any proteasomal pathway?
qanda.a: (Refs: Brownell 2010; Duda 2008.) Expected answer: non-neddylated cullins cannot reach the active Rbx1 conformation, so CRL activity — and only CRL activity — is abolished; HECT/RBR/RING-only E3 ligases are unaffected; stabilization by pevonedistat narrows the responsible E3 to the ~250-member CRL family.

qanda.q: What are the key controls for a K-εGG diGly proteomics experiment, and how would you distinguish true ubiquitination from NEDD8 or ISG15 sites that also leave a diGly remnant?
qanda.a: (Refs: Kim 2011; Xu 2010; Udeshi 2013.) Expected answer: include ± MG132 to confirm proteasome dependence, use His-Ub pulldown as orthogonal specificity check, compare to NAE-inhibited (pevonedistat) condition to exclude NEDD8 sites; ISG15 sites are rare under basal conditions and distinguishable by differential response to IFN treatment.

qanda.q: Why would you choose TurboID over BioID to map the in vivo DCAF7 interactome in MPN HSCs?
qanda.a: (Refs: Roux 2012; Branon 2018.) Expected answer: BioID requires 18-24 hr biotin labeling, infeasible in vivo; TurboID labels in 10-30 min with low-toxicity biotin, enabling labeling after transplantation of TurboID-DCAF7-transduced HSPCs into mice that have established MPN.

qanda.q: Your Mx1-Cre × Dcaf7^F/F^ model assumes efficient recombination in LT-HSCs. How would you quantify this at single-cell resolution and distinguish a true HSC phenotype from an escaper-cell artifact?
qanda.a: (Refs: Soriano 1999; Muzumdar 2007.) Expected answer: crossing in a Rosa26-YFP (R26R-EYFP) or mT/mG reporter allows flow-cytometric quantification of recombined (YFP^+^ or mG^+^) fraction in CD150^+^CD48^−^ LSK at serial timepoints; declining recombined fraction over time indicates fitness disadvantage, whereas stable fraction argues the phenotype is not confounded by escaper outgrowth.

qanda.q: What does dTAG-mediated degradation offer that your Dcaf7^F/F^ Cre-inducible model does not?
qanda.a: (Ref: Nabet 2018.) Expected answer: acute (hours) rather than days-to-weeks protein loss, distinguishing immediate from adaptive DCAF7-loss phenotypes; reversibility by ligand washout enables on-off-on experiments; chemical control avoids poly(I:C)-associated IFN confounds entirely.

qanda.q: How does your methodology scale from mouse to human, and why is CRISPR-RNP in primary CD34^+^ cells the current standard for such bridging?
qanda.a: (Ref: Tothova 2017.) Expected answer: RNP electroporation achieves 40-80% editing without extended culture (which degrades HSC quality), outperforms lentiviral Cas9, allows multiplex editing of DCAF7 ± IFIT3 ± JAK2V617F, and supports NSG transplant readouts of long-term repopulating activity at 8-12 weeks.
